_meta.story_format: As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit]
notes: SEED file — replaced once the wbs-breakdown skill runs on module M1.
Demonstrates the v1.0 schema, incl. a cross-module blocker (People utilization ← PM workload) and an open external_dep flag.

out_of_scope.name: Admin
out_of_scope.reason: Out of scope for this WBS

modules.name: People
modules.description: Workforce management — headcount, org, allocation, performance, lifecycle

sub_modules.name: Dashboard
sub_modules.description: Workforce overview — headcount, utilization, attrition, skill gap, forecast

screens.name: Org / Workforce Dashboard
screens.description: Full workforce metrics view for leadership and management roles

sub_screens.name: KPI Strip
sub_screens.description: Headcount, utilization %, bench, attrition with sparklines

items.title: View company-wide workforce KPI strip
items.story: As an Admin/BOD, I want to see a real-time KPI strip showing total headcount, utilization %, bench count, and attrition rate with sparkline trends so that I can instantly assess overall workforce health.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an Admin/BOD, when the dashboard loads, then the KPI strip shows headcount, utilization %, bench count, and attrition rate, each with a sparkline trend.
Given a metric has no data for the period, when the strip renders, then an empty state is shown instead of a misleading zero.
items.notes: AM sees this scoped to their own accounts only.

items.title: Role-based data scoping for workforce metrics
items.story: Provide a shared role/account scoping layer so every workforce view returns only the data the current role is permitted to see.
acceptance_criteria: Given any workforce query, when executed, then results are filtered to the caller's permitted accounts/projects.
Given an AM with no extra grants, when they load metrics, then only their assigned accounts are returned.
items.notes: Underpins every scoped view across the People module.

modules.name: Project Monitoring
modules.description: PM module — project allocation, workload, delivery health. Owns the workload data People consumes.

sub_modules.name: Workload
sub_modules.description: Per-person allocation across projects — the source of utilization and bench data

screens.name: Allocation Board
screens.description: Project managers assign and balance people across projects

items.title: Workload aggregation service
items.story: Provide an aggregation service that computes each employee's allocation/utilization across all their project assignments.
acceptance_criteria: Given a set of project allocations, when queried for an employee, then total allocated % and remaining capacity are returned.
Given an employee on no project, when queried, then they are reported as benched.
items.notes: Owned by PM module; the People dashboard's utilization KPI depends on this.

items.title: See available bench when staffing a project
items.story: As a PMO, I want to see who is currently on the bench when I staff a project so that I can allocate idle people first.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open the allocation board, when I add a role, then a list of benched, skill-matching employees is suggested.
Given no benched employee matches, when I add a role, then an empty state explains why.

external_deps.reason: bench status is derived in the People module; reconcile must resolve this to the concrete People item once it exists.
